TO WE'D THIS EVENING.
Miss Ethel M. Lindsay, daughter
of Mr.. and Mrs. F. T. Lindsay, and
J. F.. Terry, son of Mr.. and Mrs.
w. M. Terry, will wed this evening
at the home of Father Butler OF
Taonzaga. Only immediate relatives
jsill be present.


4 3


WEDDING DATE SET.


The nuptials of Miss Helen Sum-
mers, daughter of Mr.. and Mrs.
Harry Summers, and James Clark.
son of Mr.. and Mrs. Patrick Clark,
will be held on December in the
early morning at the cathedral OF
Our Lady of Lourdes.


3 4 -


POSTAL CLERKS WILL DANCE.
An event of much local interest
to government employees will be
the dance given by the National
Association of Postoffice Clerks at
the Elks' temple. Tuesday evening,
November %. The guests of hon.
or will be the retiring officers, j.
O. Walstrom, G. c. Wilt, c. A. AS
kins and p. r. Ball.


WoMAN's CLUB WILL STUDY


COMPOSERS.


At the next meeting of the
Womans club the members will
make study of women composers.
A very interesting meeting was
held yesterday afternoon at Hotel
223. A musical program including
vocal and instrumental selections
and dances, was rendered by mem
bers of the club.


PAN HELLENlc's MEETING


THURSDAY.


The Pan Hellenic association
will be entertained at the home OF
Mrs. f. B. Macon, 211 Euclid ave-
nue.. on Thursday afternoon.
UNITE ELMiRA YOUNG PEOPLE
Miss Hazel M. Cannady and Rob
ert e. Lea, OF Elmira. Wash, were
united In marriage at the Pilgrim
CONGREGATIONAL church yesterday
by the Rev. w. J. Hindley.

ANNOUNCE ENGAGEMENT.
Mr.. and Mrs. James Durkin an
nounce the engagement of their
daughter Eva to Leo Jones, the
son of Mr.. and Mrs. II. 7. Jones OF
Olympia. Wash.


WILL HAVE HOUSE WARMING


ON MONDAY NIGHT.


Members of the Woman's club.
now building the first Spokane
club house to be erected by the
women of this city will hold a
house warming in their new home
next Monday evening. The new
club house Is OH Walnut street near
Ninth avenue. 1t is not finished on
the interior as yet. Some time IN
the future when the cottage has
been fully decorated and furnished
a larger affair will be held, at
which time members OF other
clubs of the city will be invited to
inspect the home.


Mr.. and Mrs. Robert l.. Dalke
and family will leave for New York
city / nthe near future for a trip.
after which they will journey to
California for the winter months.

DR.. and Mrs. Thomas A. Russell
are expected home from Portland
this week.


Mrs. A. B. Conkling, who has
been the guest of Mrs. II. b. Gray
bill and Mrs. A. Il. House for the
past few days. has returned to her
home In Conklin Park, Idaho.
